Study Summary

WHAT THE STUDY INVOLVES:

  • ~2 hours of time at your computer (this may be longer depending on how you answer) for five different sessions. You will automatically be directed to each new part but you can take breaks by sending yourself the survey link.

  • Signing the below consent form, correctly answering the consent quiz, and completing an eligibility survey

  • SMS verification of a unique phone number
  • A 24 - 48hr wait while researchers ensure eligibility 

  • 2 long questionnaires about psychedelic and other drug use, mental health, how you think, and any unusual sensory experiences you've had.

  • 4 online games (10-25 minutes each)

  • A few debriefing and quality-control questions.

  • You will repeat the same games but answer fewer questionnaires for each subsequent timepoint:

COMPENSATION:

  • $50 via Amazon gift card (emailed) for every timepoint ($250 total)

RULES FOR PARTICIPATION:

  • Payment may be withheld until you replay the games if your responses show that you did not understand the rules or answered randomly.
  • Payment may be withheld if survey responses show you are not paying attention, have provided fraudulent responses, or are likely to be taking the survey multiple times.
  • Payment may be withheld if you fail to complete all the games/questionnaires within the time limit for each timepoint (72 hr for baseline)

WHAT IS NEEDED TO PARTICIPATE:

  1. A Computer (not smartphone or tablet)

  2. Stable internet

  3. Good headphones

  4. A private, distraction-free space

  5. Plan and ability to safely use a psychedelic before October of 2026

  6. No serotonergic or atypical psychedelic use in the past 6 weeks

  7. No same-day psychoactive drug-use (besides nicotine or caffeine)

  8. A cellphone that can receive SMS texts
  9. Willingness to abstain from other serotonergic or atypical psychedelic use (besides the single planned session) until the final time point is completed (minimum 1 month after your next serotonergic psychedelic use)

Section Header:
Module: Thoughts on Voices & Experiences

 

REMINDER:

We understand that the phrase "the voices" or "my voices" can seem stigmatizing or lead to labeling. We also understand that for many, "the voices" or "my voices" really means "Spirit," "My Higher Power," "My Higher Self," "My Guides," "the Universe," "My Inner Guidance," "Aspects" and so much more. 

Because we have so many different groups participating in this study, we had to use the most general term of "voices." However, we encourage you to replace the phrase "my voices" or "the voices" in your mind while reading the question to whatever phrase reflects your beliefs.

Section Header: Module: Serotonergic Psychedelic Use
Field Label:
In this section, you will be asked about your substance use history.

Some questions may seem repetitive at times. Please pay close attention and make sure your responses are consistent across different questions. Although this is the survey is focused on  serotonergic psychedelics, we also need to asses people's lifetime history with other substances to understand the effect of just serotonergic psychedelics.

Please respond truthfully, as your answers will be kept strictly confidential and will not be shared with anyone outside of the research team.

Section Header:
Your Questionnaires Progress:


 
20%
Field Label:
DOSE GUIDE:

LSD

Psilocybin Mushrooms (cubensis; dry)

Mescaline (pure)

5-MeO-DMT (pure)

DMT (pure)

Threshold
Also called a Microdose. A threshold dose is the dose at which the mental and physical alterations produced by the substance first become apparent. These effects are distinctly beyond that of placebo but are easily ignored by directing one's focus towards the external environment. Subjects may perceive a vague sense of "something" or anticipatory energy building up in the background at this level.

Light
A light dose produces a state which is fully distinct from sobriety but does not threaten to override the subject's ordinary awareness. The effects can be ignored by increasing the focus one directs towards the external environment and performing complex tasks. The subject may have to pay particular attention for the substance's effects to be perceptible, or they may be slightly noticeable but will not insist upon the subject's attention.

Common
A common dose is the dose at which the effects and nature of the substance is quite clear and distinct; the subject's ordinary awareness slips and ignoring its action becomes difficult. The subject will generally be able to partake in regular behaviors and remain functional and able to communicate, although this can depend on the individual. The effects can be allowed to occupy a predominant role or they may be suppressed and made secondary to other chosen activities with sufficient effort or in case of an emergency.

Strong
A strong dose renders its subject mostly incapable of functioning, interacting normally, or thinking in a straightforward manner. The effects of the substance are clear and can no longer be ignored or suppressed, leaving the subject entirely engaged in the experience regardless of their desire or volition. Negative effects become more common at this level. As subjects are not able to alter the trajectory of their behavior at strong doses, it is vital that they have prepared their environment and activities in advance as well as taken any precautionary measures.

Heavy
A heavy dose is the upper limit of what a substance is capable of producing in terms of psychoactive effects. Depending on the substance consumed, the user may be rendered incapable of functioning and communicating in addition to experiencing extremely uncomfortable side effects that overshadow the positive effects experienced at lower doses. Any dose that engenders out-of-body experiences and/or dream-like experiences where one fully cannot access/perceive the environment of consensual reality in which they took the drug should be considered "Heavy"!!

Field Label:
Participants who have used psychedelics have reported fascinating, non-distressing hallucination-like visual experiences after using psychedelics (eg. breathing objects, moving patterns, more visual intensity, etc.).
We want to know about other sensory experiences people may have after using psychedelics outside just what they see.
Have you ever noted NEW unusual experiences in your HEARING (sounds, noises, audition, etc.) that started AFTER a psychedelic experience, when you were NO LONGER high?
This does NOT count times when (1) you were under the influence of another psychoactive drug or (2) you were in a trance, falling asleep, waking up, or had not slept in a long time, or (3) you were experiencing known symptoms of a psychotic spectrum disorder (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, schizoaffective disorder, schizophreniform disorder, etc.)

Please list, in a numbered list, any changes you've had in your HEARING (sounds, noises, audition, etc.) in the days, weeks, or years after psychedelic use. Please provide as much detail surroudning the experience as you are willing to offer with regard to what the experience was, what triggered it, how long it lasted, how frequent it was, and how long you've had experiences like it since psychedelic use. You may also include additional details that you think may be worth noting. Please just include each on its own numbered line. 
    For example: 
    1. Processed foods tasted worse. This started after LSD when I was 22 and is present now. It is constant. 
    2. I randomly tasted metal three times after one DMT experience at 45. The last time this happened was 4 days after the DMT. It came out of the blue and lasted for 1-5 seconds...
    3. Etc.

Section Header:
Difficult Life Experiences

Yale students -- skip this section.

You are doing AMAZING! Thank you again for all of the hard work you are continuing to put into this study!

We want to understand how psychedelics might cause different positive and negative effects in different people. One variable that may be critical to consider is one's history of traumatic life experiences. We know that it is often painful to talk about trauma, and so we only require a single question just to know -- in the vaguest sense possible -- if you have every experienced any trauma at three points in your life.

If you feel comfortable sharing more information about your experiences, then you can optionally do so, and we will put the information towards research designed to help people experiencing mental illness, including trauma-induced illness.

Regardless, we want you to know that there many other resources out there for you if you feel like you need someone to talk to or peers to connect with. Here are some great resources that are all over the U.S. and other areas of the world:

U.S. and Canada:

https://www.traumasurvivorsnetwork.org/pages/our-centers

https://www.mhanational.org/find-support-groups

https://www.nami.org/Learn-More/Mental-Health-Conditions/Posttraumatic-Stress-Disorder/Support


International:

https://www.colleaga.org/article/list-ptsd-organizations-and-resources
Field Label: Have you EVER experienced a traumatic experience?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes|2, No|3, Unsure -- can I please see a definition of "trauma" and common (non-explicit) examples?

Field Label: At what points in your life have you experienced a trauma?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Before I ever used psychedelics|2, After I used psychedelics|3, Childhood (before the age of 18)

Field Label:
A traumatic experience is an event, series of events, or set of circumstances that is experienced by an individual as physically or emotionally harmful or life threatening and that has lasting adverse effects on the individual's functioning and mental, physical, social, emotional, or spiritual well-being.
-Examples of traumatic experiences include, but are not limited to: 
-Experiencing or observing physical, sexual, and emotional abuse
-Childhood neglect
-Having a family member with a mental health or substance use disorder
-Experiencing or witnessing violence in the community or while serving in the military
-Poverty and systemic discrimination.

Are you OK with telling us what kinds of traumatic experiences you've had at each stage of life?  (Going through a list of common types of trauma and hitting 'yes' or 'no' and then telling us when it has happened to you if 'yes'). This is ENTIRELY OPTIONAL. Thank you so much for your participation!

Field Label: Have you completed the "Can You See the Pattern?" Game but it's still not letting you move on?

FIRST you should try clicking the link again and waiting 5 seconds to see if itIf you want to finish this survey as soon as possible the best option is to replay the game (we're sorry!) Just make sure you have stable internet first!

 

If you most want to avoid replaying the game there is one way to check to see if you can recover the data and move on. 

 

Hit Yes if you want to see this method. 

 

if either approach fails, you may email maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu with the subject line "Task data not saving for [record_id]" and include in the body of the email which game you are on and the date and time you completed the game.

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the little box that pops up
  4. Enter (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('vch_short_psychedelic_bl')) -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" before doing so! 

  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again. 

  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the giant blob of text into the box below!
task_data_vch_bl_retrieved

If you got the task data, paste it here and you can move on! MAKE SURE YOU COPY AND PASTE THE DATA CAREFULLY! Omitting a single character will result in us being unable to interpret your data and you needing to replay :( Remember! All answers are reviewed in detail before payment can be made!

  1. Right click

  3. Select the "console" tab from the little box that pops up
  4. Enter (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('ach_task_short_baseline')) -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" before doing so! 

  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.

Field Label:
Participants who have used psychedelics have reported fascinating, non-distressing hallucination-like visual experiences after using psychedelics (eg. breathing objects, moving patterns, more visual intensity, etc.).
We want to know about other sensory experiences people may have after using psychedelics outside just what they see.
Have you ever noted NEW unusual experiences in your HEARING (sounds, noises, audition, etc.) that started AFTER a psychedelic experience, when you were NO LONGER high?
This does NOT count times when (1) you were under the influence of another psychoactive drug or (2) you were in a trance, falling asleep, waking up, or had not slept in a long time, or (3) you were experiencing known symptoms of a psychotic spectrum disorder (schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, schizoaffective disorder, schizophreniform disorder, etc.)

Please list, in a numbered list, any changes you've had in your HEARING (sounds, noises, audition, etc.) in the days, weeks, or years after psychedelic use. Please provide as much detail surroudning the experience as you are willing to offer with regard to what the experience was, what triggered it, how long it lasted, how frequent it was, and how long you've had experiences like it since psychedelic use. You may also include additional details that you think may be worth noting. Please just include each on its own numbered line. 
    For example: 
    1. Processed foods tasted worse. This started after LSD when I was 22 and is present now. It is constant. 
    2. I randomly tasted metal three times after one DMT experience at 45. The last time this happened was 4 days after the DMT. It came out of the blue and lasted for 1-5 seconds...
    3. Etc.

Section Header:
Difficult Life Experiences

You are doing AMAZING! Thank you again for all of the hard work you are continuing to put into this study!

We want to understand how psychedelics might cause different positive and negative effects in different people. One variable that may be critical to consider is one's history of traumatic life experiences. We know that it is often painful to talk about trauma, and so we only require a single question just to know -- in the vaguest sense possible -- if you have every experienced any trauma at three points in your life.

If you feel comfortable sharing more information about your experiences, then you can optionally do so, and we will put the information towards research designed to help people experiencing mental illness, including trauma-induced illness.

Regardless, we want you to know that there many other resources out there for you if you feel like you need someone to talk to or peers to connect with. Here are some great resources that are all over the U.S. and other areas of the world:

U.S. and Canada:

https://www.traumasurvivorsnetwork.org/pages/our-centers

https://www.mhanational.org/find-support-groups

https://www.nami.org/Learn-More/Mental-Health-Conditions/Posttraumatic-Stress-Disorder/Support


International:

https://www.colleaga.org/article/list-ptsd-organizations-and-resources
Field Label: Have you EVER experienced a traumatic experience?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Yes|2, No|3, Unsure -- can I please see a definition of "trauma" and common (non-explicit) examples?

Field Label: At what points in your life have you experienced a trauma?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Before I ever used psychedelics|2, After I used psychedelics|3, Childhood (before the age of 18)

Field Label: Acceptance and Action Questionnaire Total Score
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: [aaq1_addon_bl]+[aaq2_addon_bl]+[aaq3_addon_bl]+[aaq4_addon_bl]+[aaq5_addon_bl]+[aaq6_addon_bl]+[aaq7_addon_bl]

Field Label:
A traumatic experience is an event, series of events, or set of circumstances that is experienced by an individual as physically or emotionally harmful or life threatening and that has lasting adverse effects on the individual's functioning and mental, physical, social, emotional, or spiritual well-being.
-Examples of traumatic experiences include, but are not limited to: 
-Experiencing or observing physical, sexual, and emotional abuse
-Childhood neglect
-Having a family member with a mental health or substance use disorder
-Experiencing or witnessing violence in the community or while serving in the military
-Poverty and systemic discrimination.

Are you OK with telling us what kinds of traumatic experiences you've had at each stage of life?  (Going through a list of common types of trauma and hitting 'yes' or 'no' and then telling us when it has happened to you if 'yes'). This is ENTIRELY OPTIONAL. Thank you so much for your participation!

You can still complete the games tomorrow, 1-2 weeks after, and 1-6 months after if you use these psychedelic types. 

It is important that there be some drug effect during the entire hour or so of games.

To the best of your knowledge, what dose of the psychedelic are you taking today? (guides for units to use below)

If you have no idea, you may search your drug at "psychonautwiki.org" and look at the right side of the page towards the top to see typical doses for different levels of effect.

Psilocybin mushrooms (mushrooms): grams of dried mushrooms OR grams of mushrooms

Psilocybin (pure): milligrams (mg)

LSD: micrograms (ug)

DMT: milligrams (mg)

Mescaline (Peyote or San Pedro): grams of dried peyote/san pedro OR grams of peyote/san pedro

Mescaline (pure): milligrams (mg)

Ayahusaca: milliliters (mL) of liquid

DOI: milligrams (mg)


2-CB: milligrams (mg)

Please estimate the subjective strength of the dose you took today using the guide below.

Threshold
Also called a Microdose. A threshold dose is the dose at which the mental and physical alterations produced by the substance first become apparent. These effects are distinctly beyond that of placebo but are easily ignored by directing one's focus towards the external environment. Subjects may perceive a vague sense of "something" or anticipatory energy building up in the background at this level.

Light
A light dose produces a state which is fully distinct from sobriety but does not threaten to override the subject's ordinary awareness. The effects can be ignored by increasing the focus one directs towards the external environment and performing complex tasks. The subject may have to pay particular attention for the substance's effects to be perceptible, or they may be slightly noticeable but will not insist upon the subject's attention.

Common
A common dose is the dose at which the effects and nature of the substance is quite clear and distinct; the subject's ordinary awareness slips and ignoring its action becomes difficult. The subject will generally be able to partake in regular behaviors and remain functional and able to communicate, although this can depend on the individual. The effects can be allowed to occupy a predominant role or they may be suppressed and made secondary to other chosen activities with sufficient effort or in case of an emergency.

Strong
A strong dose renders its subject mostly incapable of functioning, interacting normally, or thinking in a straightforward manner. The effects of the substance are clear and can no longer be ignored or suppressed, leaving the subject entirely engaged in the experience regardless of their desire or volition. Negative effects become more common at this level. As subjects are not able to alter the trajectory of their behavior at strong doses, it is vital that they have prepared their environment and activities in advance as well as taken any precautionary measures.

Heavy
A heavy dose is the upper limit of what a substance is capable of producing in terms of psychoactive effects. Depending on the substance consumed, the user may be rendered incapable of functioning and communicating in addition to experiencing extremely uncomfortable side effects that overshadow the positive effects experienced at lower doses. Any dose that engenders out-of-body experiences and/or dream-like experiences where one fully cannot access/perceive the environment of consensual reality in which they took the drug should be considered "Heavy"!!
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Threshold|2, Light|3, Common|4, Strong|5, Heavy

Section Header:
IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS PLEASE READ CAREFULLY!!!

Please begin the games after the peak effects -- as soon as you feel you are able to focus and complete the tasks you did at baseline with reasonable accuracy.

We recommend you please set an alarm based on the below timings:

(if you are taking a different drug please use psychonaut.wiki.org to estimate peak effect timing!)
Psilocybin: 150 - 210 minutes (2.5 - 3.5 hours)
LSD: 180 - 300 minutes (3 - 5 hours)
Ayahuasca (or oral DMT + MAOI): ~100 minutes (1 hour 40 minutes)
Mescaline: 300 - 360 minutes (~5 - 6 hours)
DOI: 3 hours

If you are taking a different drug please go to https://psychonautwiki.org/wiki/Main_Page and search your drug; you will find a table with timing of effects on the right hand side towards the top of the page -- use the row "Peak".

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('vch_short_psychedelic_2')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!

If you have no idea, you may search your drug at "psychonautwiki.org" and look at the right side of the page towards the top to see typical doses for different levels of effect.

DOI: milligrams (mg)

Please estimate the subjective strength of the dose you took yesterday using the guide below.

Threshold
Also called a Microdose. A threshold dose is the dose at which the mental and physical alterations produced by the substance first become apparent. These effects are distinctly beyond that of placebo but are easily ignored by directing one's focus towards the external environment. Subjects may perceive a vague sense of "something" or anticipatory energy building up in the background at this level.

Light
A light dose produces a state which is fully distinct from sobriety but does not threaten to override the subject's ordinary awareness. The effects can be ignored by increasing the focus one directs towards the external environment and performing complex tasks. The subject may have to pay particular attention for the substance's effects to be perceptible, or they may be slightly noticeable but will not insist upon the subject's attention.

Common
A common dose is the dose at which the effects and nature of the substance is quite clear and distinct; the subject's ordinary awareness slips and ignoring its action becomes difficult. The subject will generally be able to partake in regular behaviors and remain functional and able to communicate, although this can depend on the individual. The effects can be allowed to occupy a predominant role or they may be suppressed and made secondary to other chosen activities with sufficient effort or in case of an emergency.

Strong
A strong dose renders its subject mostly incapable of functioning, interacting normally, or thinking in a straightforward manner. The effects of the substance are clear and can no longer be ignored or suppressed, leaving the subject entirely engaged in the experience regardless of their desire or volition. Negative effects become more common at this level. As subjects are not able to alter the trajectory of their behavior at strong doses, it is vital that they have prepared their environment and activities in advance as well as taken any precautionary measures.

Heavy
A heavy dose is the upper limit of what a substance is capable of producing in terms of psychoactive effects. Depending on the substance consumed, the user may be rendered incapable of functioning and communicating in addition to experiencing extremely uncomfortable side effects that overshadow the positive effects experienced at lower doses. Any dose that engenders out-of-body experiences and/or dream-like experiences where one fully cannot access/perceive the environment of consensual reality in which they took the drug should be considered "Heavy"!!
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Threshold|2, Light|3, Common|4, Strong|5, Heavy

task_data_vch_retrieved_2_acu

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('vch_short_psychedelic_2')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(sessionStorage.getItem('PRLtask1taskData')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!

Please estimate the subjective strength of the dose you took 1-2 weeks ago using the guide below.

Threshold
Also called a Microdose. A threshold dose is the dose at which the mental and physical alterations produced by the substance first become apparent. These effects are distinctly beyond that of placebo but are easily ignored by directing one's focus towards the external environment. Subjects may perceive a vague sense of "something" or anticipatory energy building up in the background at this level.

Light
A light dose produces a state which is fully distinct from sobriety but does not threaten to override the subject's ordinary awareness. The effects can be ignored by increasing the focus one directs towards the external environment and performing complex tasks. The subject may have to pay particular attention for the substance's effects to be perceptible, or they may be slightly noticeable but will not insist upon the subject's attention.

Common
A common dose is the dose at which the effects and nature of the substance is quite clear and distinct; the subject's ordinary awareness slips and ignoring its action becomes difficult. The subject will generally be able to partake in regular behaviors and remain functional and able to communicate, although this can depend on the individual. The effects can be allowed to occupy a predominant role or they may be suppressed and made secondary to other chosen activities with sufficient effort or in case of an emergency.

Strong
A strong dose renders its subject mostly incapable of functioning, interacting normally, or thinking in a straightforward manner. The effects of the substance are clear and can no longer be ignored or suppressed, leaving the subject entirely engaged in the experience regardless of their desire or volition. Negative effects become more common at this level. As subjects are not able to alter the trajectory of their behavior at strong doses, it is vital that they have prepared their environment and activities in advance as well as taken any precautionary measures.

Heavy
A heavy dose is the upper limit of what a substance is capable of producing in terms of psychoactive effects. Depending on the substance consumed, the user may be rendered incapable of functioning and communicating in addition to experiencing extremely uncomfortable side effects that overshadow the positive effects experienced at lower doses. Any dose that engenders out-of-body experiences and/or dream-like experiences where one fully cannot access/perceive the environment of consensual reality in which they took the drug should be considered "Heavy"!!
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Threshold|2, Light|3, Common|4, Strong|5, Heavy

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('vch_short_psychedelic_2')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!

If you have no idea, you may search your drug at "psychonautwiki.org" and look at the right side of the page towards the top to see typical doses for different levels of effect.
Psilocybin mushrooms: grams of dried mushrooms OR grams of mushrooms
Psilocybin (pure): milligrams (mg)
LSD: micrograms (ug)
DMT: milligrams (mg)
Mescaline (Peyote or San Pedro): grams of dried peyote/san pedro OR grams of peyote/san pedro
Mescaline (pure): milligrams (mg)
Ayahusaca: milliliters (mL)
DOI: milligrams (mg)

Please estimate the subjective strength of the dose you took 1-6 months ago using the guide below.

Threshold
Also called a Microdose. A threshold dose is the dose at which the mental and physical alterations produced by the substance first become apparent. These effects are distinctly beyond that of placebo but are easily ignored by directing one's focus towards the external environment. Subjects may perceive a vague sense of "something" or anticipatory energy building up in the background at this level.

Light
A light dose produces a state which is fully distinct from sobriety but does not threaten to override the subject's ordinary awareness. The effects can be ignored by increasing the focus one directs towards the external environment and performing complex tasks. The subject may have to pay particular attention for the substance's effects to be perceptible, or they may be slightly noticeable but will not insist upon the subject's attention.

Common
A common dose is the dose at which the effects and nature of the substance is quite clear and distinct; the subject's ordinary awareness slips and ignoring its action becomes difficult. The subject will generally be able to partake in regular behaviors and remain functional and able to communicate, although this can depend on the individual. The effects can be allowed to occupy a predominant role or they may be suppressed and made secondary to other chosen activities with sufficient effort or in case of an emergency.

Strong
A strong dose renders its subject mostly incapable of functioning, interacting normally, or thinking in a straightforward manner. The effects of the substance are clear and can no longer be ignored or suppressed, leaving the subject entirely engaged in the experience regardless of their desire or volition. Negative effects become more common at this level. As subjects are not able to alter the trajectory of their behavior at strong doses, it is vital that they have prepared their environment and activities in advance as well as taken any precautionary measures.

Heavy
A heavy dose is the upper limit of what a substance is capable of producing in terms of psychoactive effects. Depending on the substance consumed, the user may be rendered incapable of functioning and communicating in addition to experiencing extremely uncomfortable side effects that overshadow the positive effects experienced at lower doses. Any dose that engenders out-of-body experiences and/or dream-like experiences where one fully cannot access/perceive the environment of consensual reality in which they took the drug should be considered "Heavy"!!
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Threshold|2, Light|3, Common|4, Strong|5, Heavy

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('vch_short_psychedelic_2')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!

task_data_ach_retrieved_2_pers

Steps for retrieving a backup of your data :

  1. Right click
  2.  Select "inspect"
  3. Select the "console" tab from the top of the little box that pops up
  4. Type (at the bottom of the box -- your cursor should already be there) console.log(localStorage.getItem('ach_task_short_psychedelic2')) and press enter -- Note: if you are trying to copy and paste this into the console you will need to type "allow pasting" and hit enter before doing so! 
  5. If you see a bunch of test saying 'survey_url', 'response', 'encrypted_metadata' then success! This is your data. If not -- sorry :( you probably lost connection during the game and need to play again.
  6. If you do have the data, just copy it (you can click and drag but better is to hit the little copy button that should be at the end)
  7. Then paste the entire blob of text into the box below!
